statement.markdown: Implement a function called `insertAt` that inserts an element at a given index in an array. The function should handle cases where the index is out of range, and return the original array in such cases. The function should return the modified array with the element inserted at the specified index.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an array and an index (integer), followed by an element to be inserted.
statement.outputFormat: The modified array with the element inserted at the specified index, or the original array if the index is out of range.
statement.notes: The array should be modified in-place.

hints.text: Check if the index is within the array bounds.

editorial.title: Editorial for Simple Array Inserter
editorial.intuition: Think about how you would insert an element at a given index in an array.
editorial.bruteForce: One way to solve this problem is to create a new array, copy all elements before the index, insert the element, and then copy all remaining elements.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use the `splice` method to insert the element at the specified index.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use the `splice` method to insert the element at the specified index, and return the modified array.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The `splice` method modifies the original array.
editorial.dryRun: Try inserting an element at index 2 in the array [1, 2, 3, 4, 5].
commonMistakes: Not checking if the index is out of range
